Task 5: Repository (line 3), Target Branch (line 9), Description (line 13), Files to Create and Files to Modify present, Implementation Notes (line 25), Acceptance Criteria (line 44), Test Requirements (line 52). Task 6: Repository (line 3), Target Branch (line 9), Description (line 13), Files to Modify present, Implementation Notes (line 27), Acceptance Criteria (line 46), Test Requirements (line 56).

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 6 task files: task-1-add-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-add-advisory-summary-service-method.md, task-3-add-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-add-cache-invalidation-on-advisory-ingestion.md, task-5-add-integration-tests-for-advisory-summary.md, task-6-add-threshold-query-parameter.md. 6 is within [3, 10].
All 6 tasks have '## Repository\n\ntrustify-backend' — Task 1 line 5, Task 2 line 5, Task 3 line 5, Task 4 line 5, Task 5 line 5, Task 6 line 5.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Tasks 1 and 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Tasks 1, 2, and 3. Task 6 depends on Tasks 1, 2, and 3. This forms a valid DAG: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 5, 6}, with no cycles.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (new file under existing modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ directory), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in manifest). Task 2: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in manifest). Task 3: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (new file under existing endpoints/ directory), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (exists in manifest). Task 4: Modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (exists in manifest). Task 5: Creates tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs (new file under existing tests/api/ directory), modifies tests/Cargo.toml (exists in manifest). Task 6: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (created by Task 1), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (created by Task 3), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in manifest). All paths are plausible within the repo structure.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map's changes list includes specific items: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct with fields critical, high, medium, low, total in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/', 'Add advisory_summary aggregation method to SbomService that queries sbom_advisory join table, deduplicates by advisory ID, groups by severity, and returns counts', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler with 5-minute tower-http cache configuration in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/', 'Add cache invalidation in advisory ingestion pipeline (modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs)', 'Add integration tests for the advisory-summary endpoint covering happy path, 404, deduplication, and cache behavior in tests/api/', 'Add optional ?threshold=critical query parameter'. Each item names specific files, structs, methods, and technical details.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have a Repository section containing only 'trustify-backend' — a single repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in tasks 1-6.

No invented paths outside the manifest structure.

assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks have '## Target Branch\n\nmain'. Task 1 line 7-9, Task 2 line 7-9, Task 3 line 7-9, Task 4 line 7-9, Task 5 line 7-9, Task 6 line 7-9.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map mentions the digest process in the 'Description Digest (Step 6a)' section but only describes what would happen: 'the description would be re-fetched from the Jira API, written to a temp file, and the digest computed using python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py'. The example shows a placeholder format 'sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' — not an actual 64-character hex hash. No actual digest values appear anywhere in the output files. The impact map explicitly notes 'simulated — external services not available'. There are no actual digest comments with real SHA-256 hashes.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references in Implementation Notes follow the prescribed format. Task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Module pattern: ... Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs` matching the convention's Rust module file scope.' and 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Framework: ... Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs` matching the convention's Rust module file scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs` matching the convention's Rust service file scope.' (3 conventions). Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's endpoint registration scope.' (3 conventions). Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust module file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task creates `tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs` matching the convention's test file scope.' Task 6: 'Applies: task modifies ... matching the convention's Rust handler file scope.' (2 conventions). All use the prescribed format, none use free-form prose, and no inapplicable conventions are listed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture has 'Priority: Major' (not 'Undefined'). The impact map explicitly states under 'Task Creation (Step 6a)': additional_fields includes '"priority": {"name": "Major"}'. It further explains: 'Priority "Major" is propagated because the feature has a set priority (not "Undefined").' and 'Priority "Major" propagated to all tasks (inherited from feature)' in the summary comment section.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture has 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0' (non-empty). CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'. The impact map states under 'Task Creation (Step 6a)': additional_fields includes '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]'. It explains: 'fixVersions ["RHTPA 1.5.0"] is propagated because the feature has a non-empty fixVersions array and no Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md, so fixVersion scope defaults to "both" (propagate to tasks).'

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map's 'Summary Comment (Step 6c)' section includes: 'Priority "Major" propagated to all tasks (inherited from feature)' and 'fixVersion "RHTPA 1.5.0" propagated to all tasks (inherited from feature; fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. Both inherited values are explicitly mentioned with propagation rationale.
